The key APS axiom `x ≤ □y → x ≤ ⊠y → x ≤ ⊠⊤` also holds since `□y = 2`, so it reduces to `x ≤ ⊠y → x ≤ 1`, and for all `y` one has `⊠y ≥ 1` only when `⊠⊤ = 1`, making the implication valid. With `x := 1`, we get `x ≐ ⊠⊤` because `⊠⊤ = 1`, but `⊠x = 1`'s image can be arranged to differ from `x` in the preorder-equivalence sense; in this concrete choice, checking `x ≐ ⊠x` is the only remaining verification step, and the model shape strongly suggests the existential target is satisfiable.
